title: Mapping and engineering the human cell–cell interactome - Nature Biotechnology
gist: Researchers from UCLA, USC, and Caltech propose a 'Billion Cell x Cell Project' — a systematic initiative to map and engineer how all major human cell types communicate — arguing that single-cell transcriptomics and spatial profiling have created a fundamental knowledge gap about cell-cell context that only direct pairwise functional profiling can fill. This perspective proposes new tools including Cell-Cell-seq and synthetic receptor engineering to build a functional atlas of intercellular signaling.

insights.claim: Despite transformative advances in single-cell transcriptomics and spatial profiling, a fundamental knowledge gap persists in understanding how cells influence each other in context — these technologies measure molecular states but cannot capture the functional outcomes of defined cell-cell interactions.
insights.speaker: Nature Biotechnology
insights.so_what: This gap represents a significant market opportunity for platforms and tools that directly profile pairwise cell interactions; companies building cell-cell interaction atlases may become key data infrastructure players.
insights.quote: they also reveal that there exists a fundamental knowledge gap of how cells influence each other in context

insights.claim: Existing computational methods for inferring cell-cell communication (CellChat, CellPhoneDB, SpaCcLink) rely on ligand-receptor co-expression from transcriptomic data and do not measure actual functional outcomes of cell-cell contact — a limitation that may generate large numbers of false-positive signaling predictions.
insights.speaker: Nature Biotechnology
insights.so_what: Tools inferring cell communication from gene expression alone may mislead drug target identification; experimental validation platforms that directly probe intercellular function have competitive advantage.

themes.theme: Billion Cell x Cell Project: moonshot proposal

insights.claim: The proposed 'Billion Cell x Cell Project' would systematically characterize outcomes of defined cell-cell dyads across all major human cell types and conditions — an approach analogous to the Human Genome Project or Human Cell Atlas but focused on intercellular functional interactions.
insights.speaker: Nature Biotechnology
insights.so_what: If funded at scale, this project could generate foundational datasets that power the next generation of cell-therapy and immunology drug discovery; early-mover platforms positioning for this data infrastructure will be advantaged.
insights.quote: a functional atlas of how all major human cell types communicate

insights.claim: Cell-Cell-seq, a new technology enabling systematic sequencing of physically interacting cell dyads, is a key enabling innovation cited for the Billion Cell x Cell Project — allowing high-throughput capture of transcriptional outcomes from defined pairwise interactions.
insights.speaker: Nature Biotechnology
insights.so_what: Platforms enabling Cell-Cell-seq at scale could become critical genomics infrastructure; watch for commercialization of dyad-sequencing methods as a growth area for sequencing tool companies.

themes.theme: Synthetic cell engineering for therapeutic discovery

insights.claim: The authors envision engineering cell-cell interactions — not just mapping them — using synthetic receptors, synNotch circuits, synthetic adhesion molecules, and designer cytokines to reprogram tissue-level function, directly enabling new cell therapy designs.
insights.speaker: Nature Biotechnology
insights.so_what: Synthetic cell-cell communication control is the next frontier of cell therapy engineering; companies building programmable cell-cell interaction platforms (synNotch, helixCAM, etc.) are working toward a large market.

insights.claim: Engineered CD47 has been shown to protect T cells from phagocytosis to enhance antitumor immunity, and cytokine receptor alphabet expansion can reprogram T cells into diverse functional states — demonstrating that defined cell-cell interaction engineering already produces therapeutically meaningful outcomes.
insights.speaker: Nature Biotechnology
insights.so_what: These published results validate the therapeutic utility of cell-cell interaction engineering; biotech companies combining interactome mapping with synthetic biology tools are closest to near-term clinical translation.
insights.locator: References (Yamada-Hunter et al., 2024; Zhao et al., 2025)

themes.theme: Enabling technology stack and commercialization links

insights.claim: Lead author Dino Di Carlo and UC Regents hold financial interests in Partillion Bioscience, Forcyte Biotechnologies, and Saku Biosciences — all of which develop technologies directly applicable to the Billion Cell x Cell Project, creating a disclosed conflict of interest that also signals a commercialization pathway.
insights.speaker: Nature Biotechnology
insights.so_what: The Perspective may partly serve as a market-framing exercise for commercial tools owned by the authors; investors should evaluate Partillion, Forcyte, and Saku as potential beneficiaries if the CZI-funded initiative scales.

insights.claim: CZ CELLxGENE Discover (Chan Zuckerberg Initiative) provides foundational single-cell data infrastructure with over 50 million cells aggregated, and the authors call for it and similar platforms to expand to capture cell-cell interaction data at scale.
insights.speaker: Nature Biotechnology
insights.so_what: CZI's continued investment in biological data infrastructure makes it a key funder to track for grants in interactome mapping; academic groups aligning with this priority will gain resource advantage.

insights.claim: Foundation models trained on spatial genomics data for characterizing cellular interactions via geometric self-supervised learning are already emerging, suggesting that the data infrastructure from a Billion Cell x Cell Project would directly feed next-generation AI models for cell biology.
insights.speaker: Nature Biotechnology
insights.so_what: Whoever generates the largest, most diverse cell-cell interaction dataset gains a durable data moat for training foundation cell models — a dynamic analogous to GPT training data for LLMs.
